item.title: Version 1.0.27
item.description:
**RavanSpeak 1.0.27 — Cancel works, Gemini transcription hardened**
- **Cancel now cancels immediately.** Cancelling a transcription or translation used to leave the app stuck (in-flight network requests and audio processing kept running — up to minutes for Gemini). Everything aborts instantly now: cloud requests, audio exports, the loudness pre-scan, and translation calls. No more force-quitting. - **"Gemini returned an unexpected format" fixed.** Replies are now pinned to a strict JSON schema, wrapped or cut-off replies are repaired automatically, chunks are shorter (8 min), and a bad chunk retries once on its own.
Apple Silicon · macOS 14+

item.title: Version 1.0.26
item.description:
**RavanSpeak 1.0.26 — Gemini self-healing + Reader fixes**
- **Gemini model errors fixed for good.** Google retired `gemini-2.5-pro` for newer API keys ("no longer available to new users"). The app no longer hardcodes model names — it asks Google which models YOUR key can use, picks the newest stable Pro (Best) / Flash (Fast), refreshes daily, and if a model is ever retired mid-run it automatically switches and retries. Applies to translation, transliteration, and cloud transcription. - **The sentence line under each row now follows the cards** — edit, delete, add, or rearrange cards and the row's sentence text (including the English line) rebuilds. Opening Edit and clicking Done refreshes every row. - **Empty cards no longer leave little bubbles** — cards left blank are removed when you click Done, and any previously saved empty bubbles are cleaned up at launch.
Apple Silicon · macOS 14+

item.title: Version 1.0.25
item.description:
**RavanSpeak 1.0.25 — Reader cards: colors, controls & drag**
- **New cards show the right colors.** Placeholder text on empty cards followed the app's dark mode instead of the tile color (white-on-white on light tiles). Each card now renders for its own tile brightness — including custom tile colors. - **✕ / ＋ / ≡ are always visible** — white-on-black with a white ring, legible on any tile color. - **Drag to rearrange works in the reader too** — drag any card onto another to move it, or onto the row's empty space to send it to the end of that row. Saves immediately. - **Easier dragging in Edit mode** — grab anywhere on a card (not just the tiny ≡ handle).
Apple Silicon · macOS 14+

item.title: Version 1.0.24
item.description:
**RavanSpeak 1.0.24 — undo & redo in Reader editing**
- **⌘Z undoes and ⇧⌘Z redoes** while editing a passage — typing coalesces into one step per pause; every insert, delete, drag-rearrange, and row deletion is its own step. Undo/redo buttons (↩︎ ↪︎) also live in the Edit header.
Apple Silicon · macOS 14+

item.title: Version 1.0.23
item.description:
**RavanSpeak 1.0.23 — cards only**
- **The sentence lines are gone from Edit mode** — you edit the cards, period. (Headings keep their text fields; the sentence text used for speech/export rebuilds from the cards automatically.) - **Insert cards anywhere** — right-click a card ▸ *Insert card before* / *Insert card after* / *Delete card*. The corner ＋ (insert after) and the dashed *Add card* tile remain. - **Drag to rearrange** — drag a card's ≡ handle onto another card (or onto *Add card*) to reorder, even across rows. - **New cards now match your colors** — placeholder text on empty cards was following the app's dark mode and showed white-on-white on light tiles; it now uses your card colors.
Apple Silicon · macOS 14+

item.title: Version 1.0.22
item.description:
**RavanSpeak 1.0.22 — card-first editing**
- **Edit the cards directly** — the cards are now the single source of truth. The Farsi sentence line in Edit mode is a read-only preview that rebuilds itself from the cards (the transliteration line used by exports rebuilds too). No more confusing two-way mapping. - **The English sentence stays editable** — it's a real translation, independent of the word cards. - **Add cards, not just delete** — every card has a small ＋ to insert a new card right after it, and each row ends with a dashed **Add card** tile. New cards start empty; ✕ still deletes.
Apple Silicon · macOS 14+

item.title: Version 1.0.21
item.description:
**RavanSpeak 1.0.21 — Reader polish**
- **Sentence lines and cards are now linked in Edit mode.** Type in the Farsi line and the cards re-split live (new words get empty tiles to fill); type in the Translit line and each card's transliteration updates; edit or delete a card and the sentence lines rebuild. The English sentence stays independent — it's a real translation, not word-by-word. - **Delete cards right in the reader** — hover a card and click the ✕ (right-click ▸ *Delete word* still works too). - **Punctuation sticks to its word.** Commas, periods, «» and friends no longer appear as their own cards — new passages are built that way and existing passages are cleaned up automatically.
Apple Silicon · macOS 14+

item.title: Version 1.0.20
item.description:
**RavanSpeak 1.0.20 — Reader fixes**
- **Custom colors now apply to the revealed text.** Previously the revealed layer (usually the transliteration) was always shown in the gold highlight, so changing its color appeared to do nothing. Your color now wins; without one, the gold highlight remains. - **Edit mode matches your card colors** — tiles and text in Edit use the same theme, tile colors, and text colors as the reader. - **Flip all is uniform** — one click flips every card face-up, another flips every card back, regardless of which ones you'd tapped individually.
Apple Silicon · macOS 14+

item.title: Version 1.0.19
item.description:
**RavanSpeak 1.0.19 — Reader: delete & tile colors**
- **Delete words and rows.** Right-click a word tile ▸ *Delete word*, or right-click anywhere in a sentence block ▸ *Delete row* (asks first). In **Edit** mode every word tile has an ✕ and every row a trash button. - **Custom tile colors.** The **Cards** panel now has color pickers for the **tile** and the **flipped tile**, alongside the text colors — in the app and in the HTML export. *Reset colors* returns everything to the theme defaults.
Apple Silicon · macOS 14+

item.title: Version 1.0.18
item.description:
**RavanSpeak 1.0.18 — Reader editing & card customization**
- **Edit passages.** In the reader, click **Edit** — every word tile shows its Farsi, transliteration, and English stacked and editable (sentence lines too). Fix any mistake from the on-device model; changes auto-save as you type. - **Arrange your cards.** New **Cards** button: choose which text is **Top / Middle / Bottom** (top is the big one), and which layer **tapping reveals**. - **Light or dark tiles + custom text colors** — applies in the app and in the **HTML export**. - **Live example.** The New passage page (and the Cards panel) shows a sample card — front and tapped — that updates as you change the options. - **Flip all** — flip every card at once to see the backs; click again to flip back.
Apple Silicon · macOS 14+

item.title: Version 1.0.17
item.description:
**RavanSpeak 1.0.17 — Gemini cloud transcription**
- **New transcription engine: Gemini cloud — the most accurate option for Farsi.** In the Transcribe dialog, switch the engine from *On-device (Whisper)* to *Gemini cloud*. Gemini understands the language, not just the sound, so it handles colloquial speech, names, and dialogue under music noticeably better than Whisper. - Opt-in and private by design: only the **audio** is sent to Google (never the video), only when you choose this engine, using your own Gemini API key (Settings ▸ Models & API keys). Uses Gemini 2.5 Pro with the default "Best" quality setting. - Note: Gemini's line timings are good but slightly less precise than Whisper's. Time fields remain editable, and section re-transcribe still uses Whisper.
Apple Silicon · macOS 14+

item.title: Version 1.0.16
item.description:
**RavanSpeak 1.0.16 — quality release**
Transcription: - **Quiet audio is now boosted before transcription.** Dialogue mixed under music or recorded at a low level transcribes much better — this was the main cause of missing/garbled lines on real-world video.
Translation & transliteration: - **Stronger API models by default.** The OpenAI/Gemini engines now use **GPT-4o / Gemini 2.5 Pro** — noticeably more accurate for Persian. The cheaper Fast tier (mini/Flash) is still available in Settings → Models & API keys. - **Scene continuity.** Each batch of lines now sees the previous lines' translations, so pronouns, names, and tone stay consistent across the whole video. - **Better prompts.** Lines are framed as continuous dialogue with register matching, and the transliteration standard gained more worked examples of spoken forms.
Exports: - **.txt files now include timestamps** — each line is `[00:01:23 – 00:01:26] text`.
Apple Silicon · macOS 14+

item.title: Version 1.0.15
item.description:
**RavanSpeak 1.0.15**
- **Smaller burned-in videos.** Export → *Video with subtitles* now offers **Smaller file (HEVC, recommended)** — re-encodes at the source resolution with HEVC for a much smaller file that looks the same — or **Maximum compatibility (H.264)** for the old, larger, widest-compatibility output. The saved size is shown when it finishes. - **Combined two-language subtitle files.** Merge two tracks into one SRT/VTT/TXT — e.g. **Farsi over English** or **English over Farsi transliteration** — and pick which language goes on top, just like the player overlay. - In the **editor**: Export → *Combined two-language file…* - In **bulk export**: tick *Also make a combined two-language file* and choose Top/Bottom.
Apple Silicon · macOS 14+

item.title: Version 1.0.14
item.description:
**RavanSpeak 1.0.14**
- **Bulk export now lets you choose tracks** — pick exactly what to export (e.g. Farsi original, English, Farsi transliteration) instead of all-or-original. - **Transliteration exports fixed** — the original Farsi track and the Farsi transliteration both use language "fa", so they were overwriting each other's file (you'd only get `fa` + `en`). Files are now named uniquely: transliteration is saved as `<video>.fa-translit.<ext>`.
Apple Silicon · macOS 14+

item.title: Version 1.0.13
item.description:
**RavanSpeak 1.0.13**
- **Interface zoom now actually scales** — ⌘+ / ⌘− / ⌘0 (View menu) resize the whole UI. (macOS ignores Dynamic Type, so 1.0.12's zoom did nothing; this scales the view hierarchy directly.) - **Drag moves the whole selection** — when you drag one of several selected videos into a folder, all selected ones move (previously only the dragged one did). - **Clearer sorting** — the Sort menu now shows a checkmark on the active field and an explicit **A → Z / Z → A** (ascending/descending) choice. Re-pick the active field to flip direction.
Apple Silicon · macOS 14+

item.title: Version 1.0.12
item.description:
**RavanSpeak 1.0.12**
Library & playback upgrades:
- **Bulk upload** — drop or pick a whole series at once; every file is added (a single file still opens straight in the editor). - **Multi-select** — hit **Select**, tick the videos you want, then run an action on all of them: **Transcribe & translate**, **Export**, **Move to folder**, or **Delete**. - **Drag to organize** — drag a video onto a folder to move it (or onto **All** to send it back to the top level). - **Sort** the library by **Name**, **Date added**, or **File size**. - **Space bar** plays / pauses the video (it still types a space normally when you're editing subtitle text). - **Zoom the interface** with **⌘+ / ⌘− / ⌘0** (View menu) — handy for reading Farsi at a larger size.
Apple Silicon · macOS 14+

item.title: Version 1.0.11
item.description:
**RavanSpeak 1.0.11**
- **Plain-text (.txt) export.** Export any subtitle track as a clean transcript (no timecodes) — the editor's Export menu now offers SRT / VTT / TXT. - **Bulk export.** In the Library, the **Batch** menu now has **Export subtitles…** — pick many videos, a format, and whether to export all tracks, and save them all to a folder at once.
Apple Silicon · macOS 14+

item.title: Version 1.0.10
item.description:
**RavanSpeak 1.0.10**
- **Fixed slow on-device translation/transliteration.** 1.0.9 made it far too slow and heavy on the machine. It's now back to fast batched processing, and the model is your choice: **Fast (Qwen 3B)** by default — quick and light — with **Best (Qwen 7B)** available in Settings ▸ Models & API keys if you want maximum accuracy and don't mind it being slower. The improved spelling consistency is kept either way. (For the fastest, most accurate results, the OpenAI/Gemini engines are still recommended.)
Apple Silicon · macOS 14+

item.title: Version 1.0.9
item.description:
**RavanSpeak 1.0.9**
- **Much better on-device translation & transliteration.** The free on-device engine now uses a stronger model (Qwen 7B) and processes each line with the surrounding context plus examples, so Finglish and translations are noticeably more accurate and consistent. Transliteration output is also auto-normalized to a consistent spelling (aa/ee/oo, kh/gh). - Removed the unreliable **Tiny/Small** transcription models — only **Large-v3** and **Turbo** remain.
Note: the on-device translator now downloads a larger (~4.5 GB) model on first use. For the very best phonetics you can still use the OpenAI or Gemini engines, or import an externally-made track.
Apple Silicon · macOS 14+

item.title: Version 1.0.8
item.description:
**RavanSpeak 1.0.8**
- **Gemini API engine.** You can now choose **Gemini** (alongside OpenAI and on-device) for translation and transliteration — it's excellent at producing accurate Finglish/translations from your edited Persian text. Add your key in Settings ▸ Models & API keys (get one free at aistudio.google.com).
Apple Silicon · macOS 14+

item.title: Version 1.0.7
item.description:
**RavanSpeak 1.0.7**
- **Editing subtitle times now works reliably.** Typing a new start/end time applies even on a just-added line (it commits a moment after you stop typing, not only on Return). The list also no longer scrolls away while you're paused and editing. - **Import a subtitle file as a *second* track.** Made a transliteration (Finglish) or translation elsewhere from your corrected Persian? Import → "Add as a new track" keeps your existing subtitles and adds the imported one alongside, matched to your timings — so you can show both at once (Overlay) and export each. (Previously importing replaced your subtitles.) - Clarified that in-app translation/transliteration is generated from your **edited subtitle text**, not the audio — and that OpenAI gives the best phonetics.
Apple Silicon · macOS 14+

item.title: Version 1.0.6
item.description:
**RavanSpeak 1.0.6**
- **Re-transcribe just a section.** Watching and spot a bad or missing stretch? Re-transcribe → "a section…", pick a range (presets for 30s / 1 min / 2 min / to playhead), and only those lines are redone — no need to redo the whole video. - **Relink moved videos.** If a video file moves or gets renamed, the editor shows a "Locate video…" button (and the Library has "Locate moved video…"). Your subtitles are kept. - **Resume where you left off.** Each video remembers your playback position and picks up there next time. - **Screen stays awake while watching** (including full screen) so the Mac doesn't sleep mid-video. - Skip buttons are now **15 seconds** (were 5).
Apple Silicon · macOS 14+

item.title: Version 1.0.5
item.description:
**RavanSpeak 1.0.5**
- **Interactive study guides.** The Reader's HTML export is now an interactive flip-card page: tap any word to flip it and reveal the transliteration, with Flip-all/Reset and 🔊 sentence playback. Works offline on your phone. In the app, word tiles are now tappable too. - **Export video with subtitles.** New Export → "Video with subtitles (.mp4)…" burns your subtitles (and dual-language overlay) into a shareable video you can AirDrop and watch in Photos. - **OpenAI key:** added a Save button and a reveal (show/hide) toggle. - **Better transliteration:** learner-friendly, consistent spelling (salaam, mikhaam) across subtitles and the Reader.
Apple Silicon · macOS 14+

item.title: Version 1.0.4
item.description:
**RavanSpeak 1.0.4**
- **Batch jobs.** In the Library, select multiple videos and choose **Batch** to transcribe and translate many at once. Jobs run one at a time at full quality and process in the background — watch progress in the new **Queue** tab, where you can cancel jobs. You can keep using the app while it runs.
Apple Silicon · macOS 14+

item.title: Version 1.0.3
item.description:
**RavanSpeak 1.0.3**
- **Fixed subtitle timing edits.** Editing a line's start/end time now takes effect when you click away — previously it only saved if you pressed Return, so timing changes seemed to do nothing. - **Model management.** A new Settings ▸ "Models & API keys" page shows which transcription/translation models are downloaded (with sizes), and lets you download or delete them and set your OpenAI key. The Transcribe dialog now marks each model as downloaded or not.
Apple Silicon · macOS 14+

item.title: Version 1.0.2
item.description:
**RavanSpeak 1.0.2**
New - **Reader** — paste or import English/Farsi text for an interactive, word-by-word study guide: toggle Farsi / transliteration / English, hide-and-reveal practice, spoken pronunciation, saved vocab, and HTML export. - **In-app auto-update** — RavanSpeak now checks for new versions and installs them itself. (This is the first version with auto-update; future updates won't need a manual download.) - Subtitle **import** (load an existing .srt/.vtt and edit it) and adjustable **playback speed**.
Fixes & accuracy - Fixed whole stretches of video coming back with no subtitles — transcription now covers the full audio instead of skipping quiet or music-covered speech. - Tighter, word-aligned subtitle timing. - Manual subtitle edits and added lines now reliably save and export.
Apple Silicon · macOS 14+
